title: ajuga chamaepitys subsp. laevigata
intro: Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. laevigata, also known as bugleweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The health benefits of bugleweed include its ability to regulate hormonal imbalances, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Bugleweed is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its herbal uses, and it is also used in some traditional medicine practices to treat various ailments.
